message:
test(frankenterm-core): add e2e capture+search harness + fix toon_parse collect turbofish for clippy

Two small changes in the same push; grouped because both sit in
the test/fuzz surface and are net-additive.

**1. `toon_parse.rs` clippy turbofish fix** (2 lines each way):

`fuzz/fuzz_targets/toon_parse.rs` was calling
`try_decode_from_lines(input.split('\n').map(str::to_string).collect(), None)`
and the analogous shape for `try_decode_stream_sync`. The `collect()`
calls relied on Rust's type inference from the function's signature
to resolve to `Vec<String>`. Under `-D clippy::missing_turbofish` the
inference-only call is flagged because a future signature change
would silently collect into a different container and the caller
would compile-fail at a different site. Adding `::<Vec<_>>` at both
call sites pins the collection type at the call site; no behavior
change, just explicit typing that survives signature evolution.

**2. `e2e_capture_search.rs` end-to-end harness** (247 LOC new test
file):

Adds an integration test that exercises the full pane-capture →
storage → FTS5 search pipeline end to end against a real temp-dir
backed `StorageHandle`. The existing unit tests cover each stage
in isolation; this test proves the composition.

Structure (abbreviated from the diff):

- `run_async_test(future)` helper: builds a
  `RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().enable_all().build()` runtime
  and blocks on the supplied future. Centralizing the runtime
  construction keeps each test body short and avoids per-test
  runtime-flavor drift.
- `emit_diag(event, payload)` helper: emits structured JSON-line
  diagnostics to stderr at each interesting event in the test flow
  (pane upsert, segment append, FTS index commit, search call,
  result canonicalization). That surface is the "structured
  logging + no mocks" pattern documented in the
  testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests skill — failures are
  bisectable from the log stream instead of requiring a rerun
  under a debugger.
- The main test body (present in the diff but not shown here in
  full): upserts a pane, appends several segments, commits, and
  drives a sequence of `search_with_results` calls against
  different `SearchOptions` configurations. Each call asserts both
  the success/deny verdict and the exact result-set shape; the
  canonicalization step pins the ordering so FTS5's bm25 tie-
  break behavior drift surfaces as a diff instead of a flaky test.

Runs against a real SQLite file under `TempDir` — no mocks, no
stubbed FTS — so the test exercises the actual FTS5 virtual table,
actual tokenizer, and actual query plan.
